content:
"Draft me an NDA."
That's one of the most common legal prompts people give AI.
It's also one of the worst.
Why?
Because an attorney would never start drafting an NDA without first understanding:
• Who are the parties?
• What information is being protected?
• How long should confidentiality obligations last?
• Are there customer, employee, vendor, or investor relationships involved?
• What risks matter most?
The quality of the document depends on the quality of the questions asked before the drafting begins.
CounselLab just launched a free course on Skool (https://lnkd.in/eFg4nbGQ)  that teaches business owners how to prompt AI the same way an attorney approaches legal drafting.
You'll learn a practical framework for turning a generic AI request into a customized NDA tailored to your business situation.
Better prompts → better legal drafts → better business decisions.
Join us on Skool and check out the course.
comment: Most missed part is context beyond questions—governing law, existing MSAs and dispute resolution clauses can silently conflict when AI drafts NDAs from isolated prompts
